violations.text: A pot was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

(Rotisserie chicken) was observed held at (129-130) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC reheated chicken to 165 or above Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

(Various food product) was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025
(Boxes of plantains and beverages) were observed stored on the floor throughout estblishment. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025
The handwash sink in the kitchen and restroom are lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

At the time of inspection, plantain's were observed held at 118 degrees Fahrenheit. Plant food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

Thermometers are lacking in both refrigerators. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024

The presence of is rodents not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving (next to hot-hold unit) have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
The floors behind and next to broiler have an accumulation of rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 06/14/2024
